key_insight: The paper turns LLM request routing into an online linear program, using SLO-weighted rewards and shadow prices to make admission decisions that explicitly trade off latency and throughput.
engineering_relevance: This gives LLM serving systems a principled, real-time router that can be tuned to different service objectives instead of relying on opaque heuristics.
method: Formulate routing as online linear programming with interpretable rewards and constraints for batch size and KV-cache limits.
Use a bid-price control policy: admit a request only when its SLO-weighted benefit exceeds the current shadow prices.
Track dual shadow prices online with warm-started projected first-order updates for millisecond-level decisions.
evidence_signal: In the Vidur simulator, the router was evaluated across multiple SLO regimes and reported improvements in end-to-end latency, time-to-first-token, throughput, and tail performance versus standard baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate online tracking of shadow prices and simulator-to-system transfer may be sensitive to workload variability and implementation overhead.
paper_title: Online Linear Programming for Multi-Objective Routing in LLM Serving
